utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dylan what's going on so i lift quite a bit of weights i try to stay in shape as much as i can i'm not like normal people i lift heavy weights and my elbow is extremely sore which elbow is it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: actually it's both my elbows but my right elbow is hurting me the most

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and you said you lift a lot of weights

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: did you play any sports when you were younger

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no anything you can think of primarily it was basketball baseball and football

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and did your elbows hurt at that time or is this a a new injury

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's new

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: when did it start

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: probably year and a half ago

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay on both elbows about a year and a half ago

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay have you taken anything for the pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: ibuprofen eight hundred milligrams three times a day

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and does anything make it better or worse

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: the more i use my hands or my arms the more it hurts

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay have you tried icing

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: does that give you any relief

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright is it the inside or outside of your elbows

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: inside

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: inside okay let's just do a quick physical exam here i'll take a look at your right elbow first

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: if i bend it this way up does it hurt it's your left does that hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how about this

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so pain with both flexion and extension

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: looks like you have little bit of limited range of motion on extension not on flexion though you said it hurts right here on the inside of your elbow

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so pain on the medial side with palpation

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright how about the outside

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no pain with palpation outside of the elbow you have do you have normal sensation in your fingers

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay great

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good to go

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sensation is normal to the touch

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: pulses equal in all extremities how about the left elbow same thing if i bend it this way does that hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not as much

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how about this way

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not as much

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so little bit of pain on flexion and extension little bit of limited range of motion on extension of the arm how about if you twist like you're opening a door

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so some pain with torsion and twisting supination what about pronation

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no pain with pronation on the right side

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: same thing on the left

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: pain with supination no pain with pronation

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: correct

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so dylan it took some x-rays coming in looks like you do n't have any any fractures or any bony misalignment which i expect with this kind of injury i do think that what you have is medial epicondylitis which is

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: is that golfer's elbow

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes same thing have you been golfing a lot

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well not in the past year and a half i've had this for a long time

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay also known as pictures elbow

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well i have n't been pitching either

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmmm well in any case what i'm gon na have to do is i'm gon na send you up for mri to take another look at this

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that will be our next step so we'll get you scheduled for the mri probably get you in pretty quick here since we're a private practice

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank god

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah and once you get the mri i'll know a little bit more what i'd like to do is something called a whole blood transfusion have you heard of that before

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no please tell me remind me

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah it should help with the healing of your elbow it's just a procedure we'll stick a needle in your elbow

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you do a stick needle in my elbow

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm and help with some of the healing of your elbow

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so it's kinda like dry needling then

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not at all

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: what is it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: is it is that that thing where like you take the blood out of like say my my thigh

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and then you literally inject it into my tendon

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that it activates the healing

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah that's exactly what it is

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: interesting cool

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: maybe i have heard about that

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: we've we've had some really good responses from other patients on it so hopefully i mean that should be a good solution for you since you've been having issues with this

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm excited

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah and we can hopefully get you scheduled for that in the next couple of weeks it's not not a major procedure and you should heal in the next two weeks so that wo n't be a problem especially considering that you're expecting a newborn soon we want to make sure you're all healed for that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: wow i did n't even say that

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i read it in your chart

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: man you doctors are good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah anything else going on today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: just trying to figure out how you're doing

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: very good thank you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you're welcome

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: nice to see you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you have a good day

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 53-year-old male who lifts heavy weights.
Elbow pain
The patient reports bilateral elbow pain, with the right side worse than the left.
The doctor asks about the patient's history of sports participation.
The patient played basketball, baseball, and football when he was younger.
The doctor asks if the patient had elbow pain during his youth or if this is a new injury.
The patient reports the elbow pain is a new injury.
The doctor asks about the duration of the symptoms.
The patient has had the pain for approximately 1.5 years.
The doctor asks if the patient has taken any medication for the pain.
The patient takes ibuprofen 800 mg 3 times daily.
The doctor asks about aggravating or alleviating factors.
The patient reports pain worsens with increased use of his hands or arms.
The doctor asks if the patient has tried icing the area.
The doctor asks if icing provides relief.
The patient reports icing has not been helpful.
The doctor asks about the specific location of the pain.
The patient reports the pain is on the medial aspect of the elbows.
The doctor initiates a physical examination of the right elbow.
The doctor notes pain with both flexion and extension.
The doctor notes limited range of motion on extension and confirms medial pain.
The doctor confirms pain on the medial side with palpation.
The doctor asks about pain on the lateral aspect of the elbow.
The doctor notes no pain with palpation on the lateral aspect and asks about sensation.
The doctor notes sensation is normal to light touch.
The doctor notes pulses are equal in all extremities and begins examination of the left elbow.
The doctor notes pain on flexion and extension and limited range of motion on extension of the left arm.
The doctor notes pain with supination and asks about pronation.
The doctor notes no pain with pronation on the right side.
The doctor notes pain with supination and no pain with pronation on the left side.
X-rays show no fracture or bony misalignment; the doctor assesses the condition as medial epicondylitis.
The doctor orders an MRI of the bilateral elbows.
The doctor discusses performing a whole blood transfusion to encourage healing.
The patient is expecting a newborn baby.
